recommended_flow: 1. When a user mentions a client or you detect a phone number, call get-client-context to load full context about that client.
2. Use the context (projects, meetings, quotations, pending tasks, insights) to have an informed conversation.
3. When the user wants to schedule a meeting, call schedule-meeting-from-whatsapp with the details.
4. If there are conflicts, inform the user and suggest alternative times based on the conflict details returned.
5. If the meeting is created successfully, share the Google Meet link (if virtual) and confirm the details.

get-client-context.description: Retrieves complete context about a client: profile, AI-generated insights, meeting history with summaries, projects, quotations, and pending tasks. Use this to prepare for conversations or before scheduling meetings.

client_id.description: Direct client ID if known. Either client_id or phone must be provided.

phone.description: Phone number of the client. Supports any format: local, with country code, with +, with dashes/spaces. The system normalizes and matches automatically across all known formats.

client.name: string
client.lead_stage: nuevo | contactado | en_proceso | propuesta_enviada | ganado | perdido
client.next_action: string or null - what needs to be done next
client.notes: string or null - general notes about client

insights.description: Object keyed by insight type (preference, need, behavior, feedback, general). Each contains an array of insights.

meetings.title: string
meetings.summary: string or null - AI-generated executive summary
key_points: array of strings - main discussion points
meetings.sentiment: string or null - overall meeting sentiment

projects.name: string
projects.status: lead | propuesta | negociacion | aprobado | en_desarrollo | revision | entregado | cerrado | cancelado

response_no_client.message: No se encontro un cliente con ese telefono.

schedule-meeting-from-whatsapp.description: Schedules a meeting for a client. Validates against team time blocks, work schedules, and existing meetings. Creates the event in Google Calendar (info@obzide.com) with optional Google Meet link, and saves it to the database.

phone_number.description: Phone number of the client to identify them. Same flexible format as get-client-context.

title.description: Title of the meeting. Example: 'Revision de proyecto web - ClientName'

start_time.description: Start time in HH:MM format (24h, Panama timezone). Example: '10:00'

end_time.description: End time in HH:MM format (24h, Panama timezone). Example: '11:00'

meeting_type.description: 'virtual' or 'presencial'. Virtual meetings auto-generate a Google Meet link.

attendees.description: Array of email addresses to invite as attendees.

create_meet.description: Whether to generate a Google Meet link. Only applies when meeting_type is 'virtual'.

project_id.description: Link the meeting to an existing project by its ID.

response_success.google_event_id: string or null - Google Calendar event ID
response_success.meet_link: string or null - Google Meet URL for virtual meetings

conflicts.label: string - description of the conflict
conflicts.time_range: string - the conflicting time range or explanation

response_no_client.message: No se encontro un cliente con ese numero de telefono.

response_missing_fields.message: Campos requeridos: phone_number, title, date, start_time, end_time

work_schedule.note: This schedule may be updated by admins. The API always checks the live schedule.
